action.reasoning: Observation: LPIPS improved marginally to 0.6101 but still high; predict.py falls back to default orbit for some objects (e.g. 05493...) due to renderer.forward missing rays_d arg, meaning the custom renderer call is incorrect (likely calling with only rays_o instead of rays_o, rays_d, triplane). White bg compositing not used. Analysis: Pose matching with rel_az helps only slightly; the fallback causes mismatch for those objs. Fixing the call to model.renderer(rays_o, rays_d, triplane_from_scene_codes) + handling output formats + white background for input (TripoSR likely trained with white BG) + robust error handling should stabilize and improve LPIPS. Custom camera func already has good robust up-vector fix. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with complete correct implementation (copy robust get func, fix call, add white bg composite, proper tensor-to-RGBA handling, gc/cache), test on val subset, submit_eval for updated metric and time.
